questions: For item 4, should non-React utilities such as `src/tools/video-editor/lib/drop-position.ts` share the conversion logic through a pure factory plus `useTimelineScale` wrapper, or do you only want the hook used in React consumers?

For `computeGroupEdgeResize`, should the shared helper own only the outer boundary calculation, with `TimelineCanvas` continuing to project child preview overrides separately, or should it return the full projected child result too?

success_criteria.criterion: `src/tools/video-editor/lib/resize-math.ts` exports pure `computeGroupChildBoundaryResize` and `computeGroupEdgeResize` helpers, both have direct unit tests, and both are referenced from `TimelineCanvas.tsx` and `useClipResize.ts`.

Resize sessions capture cursor-to-handle offset for `clip`, `group-child-boundary`, and `group-edge`, and the new pointer integration test shows the live boundary position matches the expected pixel during drag with no jump on release.
`TimelineCanvas` no longer maintains `resizePreviewRef` and `resizeOverrides` as two separate authoritative resize-preview states; rendered preview and commit read from one source of truth.

A shared timeline-scale abstraction exists and is used across the resize/timeline conversion sites touched by this cleanup, eliminating the duplicated `scaleWidth / scale` math in the current resize flow.
`tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit` completes without video-editor type errors after the cleanup.
The video-editor test suite passes, including the new `resize-math` unit tests and at least one end-to-end pointer resize test for interior boundary plus the group-edge variant.
The resize subsystem ends up smaller or flatter than before, with less duplicated math and fewer parallel state paths.
The extracted math helpers stay hook-free and side-effect-free, with inputs and outputs expressed only as plain data.
Manual drag verification shows the boundary feels pixel-perfect under the cursor in the original repro path.

assumptions: `useSyncExternalStore` is the preferred way to collapse preview state here because the repo already uses it and it satisfies the single-source requirement without introducing a new library.

If `useTimelineScale` cannot be used directly from pure utility files, the implementation will add a small pure scale helper under `src/tools/video-editor/lib` and keep `useTimelineScale` as the React wrapper over that shared logic.

The new pointer integration coverage can live in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` with a local stateful harness rather than requiring a broader `TimelineEditor` mount.

The listed non-preview regression paths are already covered by existing video-editor suites closely enough that rerunning the full `src/tools/video-editor/**` tests is the right verification strategy, not adding unrelated new scenario tests.
